title: elaeocarpus habbemensis
intro: Elaeocarpus habbemensis, also known as the 'Mangosteen of the mountains', is a plant species that belongs to the family Elaeocarpaceae and is native to the tropical regions of Papua New Guinea. The plant has been found to have various medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, which contribute to its potential health benefits. The health benefits of Elaeocarpus habbemensis are said to include the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, as well as its ability to lower blood sugar levels and improve cardiovascular health. Elaeocarpus habbemensis can be prepared as an herbal tea, infusion, or decoction, with the leaves and bark being used to create medicinal preparations.
